description: Mobile marketplace (Expo + RN + Supabase) where creators claim bounties from listers. v1 MVP per docs/product-plan.md §3.1, design language per docs/design.md, schema per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7. No payments, no OAuth — Apify scrapes power creator metrics.

userStories.title: Bootstrap Expo + TypeScript app skeleton
userStories.description: Initialize the Expo app at the repo root so future stories have something to build into.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `npx create-expo-app@latest . --template blank-typescript --no-install` (skip install — install with bun after)
Install deps with `bun install` (bun is available; do NOT use npm/yarn)
Add `app.json` config: name=Marketify, slug=marketify, scheme=marketify, ios.bundleIdentifier=com.marketify.app, android.package=com.marketify.app
Add `tsconfig.json` strict mode + paths: `@/*` → `src/*`
Create `src/` with empty `components/`, `screens/`, `lib/`, `types/` subfolders (each with a `.gitkeep`)
Add scripts to package.json: `typecheck` (tsc --noEmit), `lint` (expo lint)
`bun run typecheck` passes
Commit

userStories.title: Install core runtime deps (Supabase, Reanimated, Navigation, NativeWind)
userStories.description: Pre-install the libraries the rest of the build depends on so subsequent stories don't keep adding deps.
acceptanceCriteria: Install: @supabase/supabase-js, @react-native-async-storage/async-storage, react-native-url-polyfill, expo-router, react-native-reanimated, react-native-gesture-handler, react-native-svg, lucide-react-native, expo-haptics, expo-linking, expo-constants, expo-font, expo-status-bar, react-native-mmkv
Configure Reanimated babel plugin in `babel.config.js`
Configure expo-router entry point in package.json (`main: expo-router/entry`)
`bun run typecheck` passes
App boots in iOS simulator without red-screen (verify with mobile-mcp: launch + take screenshot)
Commit

userStories.title: Create Supabase project + apply baseline extensions/enums migration
userStories.description: Stand up the Supabase project and the foundational schema enums per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7.
acceptanceCriteria: Use Supabase MCP `list_organizations` then `create_project` (name=marketify-mvp, region=us-east-1) if no project exists; else use existing
Save project ref + URL + publishable key into `.env` keys SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY (do NOT print values back to chat)

Apply migration `us_003_baseline_enums`: enable extensions (pgcrypto, pg_cron if available), create enums per tech-architecture.md §4.7 (user_role, listing_status, application_status, submission_status, scrape_mode, etc.)
Run `get_advisors({type:'security'})` — note any findings (expected: low/none for enums)
Run `generate_typescript_types` → write to `src/types/supabase.ts`
Commit migration + types

userStories.title: Migration: users + creator_profiles + lister_profiles + social_links
userStories.description: Identity tables per tech-architecture.md §4.7. No Supabase Auth — custom JWT.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration `us_004_identity_tables` creates: users, creator_profiles, lister_profiles, social_links exactly per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7
Include all denormalized creator metric columns (tiktok_follower_count, instagram_avg_views_last_10, instagram_follower_count, etc.)
Foreign keys + unique constraints per spec
Regenerate TypeScript types
`get_advisors({type:'security'})` — fix any errors before commit (RLS will be added in a later story; expect a 'rls disabled' warning, that is acceptable here)
Commit

userStories.title: Migration: listings + listing_versions + pre/post conditions
userStories.description: Campaign-side tables per tech-architecture.md §4.7 including the version table the cascade trigger relies on.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration `us_005_listing_tables` creates: listings, listing_versions, listing_pre_conditions, listing_post_conditions
Include cache columns `min_followers_tiktok`, `min_followers_instagram` as plain integer (NOT generated) on listings, with btree indexes
Foreign keys, NOT NULL, defaults exactly per spec
Regenerate TypeScript types
Commit

userStories.title: Migration: applications + submissions tables
userStories.description: Pipeline tables that drive the creator/lister workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration `us_006_pipeline_tables` creates: applications, submissions per spec
Include status enums, foreign keys, the reserved revision-request column on submissions per tech-architecture.md
Regenerate TypeScript types
Commit

userStories.title: Migration: metric_snapshots + denormalization trigger
userStories.description: Snapshot table + the BEFORE INSERT trigger that mirrors values to creator_profiles, scoped by scrape_mode and serialized with pg_advisory_xact_lock.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration `us_007_metric_snapshots` creates the table with `apify_run_id` unique constraint and `scrape_mode` enum column
Includes the BEFORE INSERT trigger function that branches on scrape_mode and updates the matching denorm columns on creator_profiles
Acquires `pg_advisory_xact_lock` keyed on (user_id, scrape_mode) before mutating
Test via `execute_sql`: insert two snapshots for the same user → verify creator_profiles updates correctly
Commit

userStories.title: Migration: notifications + events audit table
userStories.description: Cross-cutting tables for in-app notifications and the append-only audit log.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration `us_008_notifications_events` creates notifications and events tables per spec
events is append-only (revoke UPDATE/DELETE)
Regenerate TypeScript types
Commit

userStories.title: Migration: RLS policies on every user-data table
userStories.description: Enable RLS + write policies per tech-architecture.md §4.7 (RLS section). JWT claims drive scoping.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration `us_009_rls_policies` enables RLS on users, creator_profiles, lister_profiles, social_links, listings, listing_versions, listing_pre_conditions, listing_post_conditions, applications, submissions, metric_snapshots, notifications, events
Policies match docs/tech-architecture.md (creator can read own + active listings; lister can manage own listings/applications; etc.)
After applying: `get_advisors({type:'security'})` returns zero error-level findings
Verification via `execute_sql` with a fake JWT claim: confirms cross-user reads are blocked
Commit

userStories.title: Migration: listing-version cascade trigger
userStories.description: Postgres trigger that bumps a version + cascades `cancelled_listing_edit` to pending applications when versioned fields change.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration `us_010_cascade_trigger` per docs/tech-architecture.md
Trigger fires on UPDATE of price, currency, max_submissions, pre_conditions, post_conditions, sample videos
Snapshots the prior state into listing_versions and updates pending applications to status=cancelled_listing_edit + emits notifications rows
Test via `execute_sql`: edit a seeded listing's price → verify a new listing_versions row exists, pending applications cascade to cancelled, notifications inserted
Commit

userStories.title: Migration: pg_cron jobs (daily refresh, hourly staleness)
userStories.description: Scheduled jobs that keep metrics fresh per docs/tech-architecture.md §3 + product-plan §3.1.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration `us_011_cron_jobs` schedules: (a) daily creator-metrics refresh job (calls the metrics-refresh edge function), (b) hourly staleness marker that flips `metrics_stale_at` after 24h
If pg_cron is not available in the project's plan, comment the migration with a TODO and add a `Spec gap:` line to progress.txt — do not silently skip
Commit

userStories.title: Seed data: one lister, one active listing, one creator with two handles
userStories.description: Minimal seed for verifying RLS + later UI work.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration `us_012_seed` (or a SQL script in `supabase/seed.sql` if Supabase prefers) inserts: 1 lister with email, 1 listing in status=active with both pre and post conditions, 1 creator with tiktok_handle + instagram_handle and fake metric_snapshots covering all three scrape_modes
Verify via `execute_sql` that creator_profiles denorm columns reflect the seeded snapshots
Commit

userStories.title: Custom JWT helper (edge function shared module)
userStories.description: Shared TS module under supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts that signs and verifies HS256 JWTs with claims (sub, role, iat, exp).
acceptanceCriteria: supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts exports signJwt(claims) and verifyJwt(token)
HS256, secret pulled from env var MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET (NOT SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET — we are signing our own)
Claims include: sub (user_id), role ('creator'|'lister'), iat, exp (7d default)
supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts exports a `corsHeaders` constant for reuse
Add MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET to .env.example
Commit

userStories.title: Edge function: auth-signup-lister
userStories.description: Listers sign up with username + email + org name → returns JWT.
acceptanceCriteria: supabase/functions/auth-signup-lister/index.ts: POST { username, email, org_name } → 200 { token, user_id, role }
Validates uniqueness on username + email; 409 on conflict
Inserts users + lister_profiles row in a single transaction
Signs JWT via _shared/jwt.ts
Deploy via `deploy_edge_function`
Round-trip test via `execute_sql` + curl-equivalent (use fetch in a tiny TS test script under scripts/test/) and confirm a row appears in users + lister_profiles
Commit

userStories.title: Apify client wrapper (edge function shared module)
userStories.description: Single place that calls Apify actors with consistent error handling. Per docs/tech-architecture.md §3.
acceptanceCriteria: supabase/functions/_shared/apify.ts exports runTikTokProfile(handle), runInstagramDetails(handle), runInstagramPosts(handle)
Each function POSTs to Apify with the actor input shape from docs/tech-architecture.md §3, includes APIFY_KEY from env
Supports a synchronous `waitSecs` parameter (default 60) so callers can await completion or fall back to webhooks
Returns { runId, datasetId, status }
Unit test (Deno test in supabase/functions/_shared/apify.test.ts) mocking fetch — verifies request shapes
Commit

userStories.title: Edge function: apify-webhook receiver (tiktok_profile branch)
userStories.description: Receive Apify run-finished webhooks, verify shared secret, fetch dataset, upsert metric_snapshots.
acceptanceCriteria: supabase/functions/apify-webhook/index.ts: POST handler verifies APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET via constant-time compare
Branch: when scrape_mode resolves to 'tiktok_profile' (passed in webhook payload metadata), parse the dataset and upsert one metric_snapshots row keyed on apify_run_id
Idempotent — duplicate webhooks for the same run_id are no-ops
Add APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET to .env.example
Test: simulate a webhook payload via fetch from a script and verify the row + denorm trigger fired
Commit

userStories.title: Edge function: apify-webhook (ig_details branch)
userStories.description: Add the IG details branch to the existing webhook handler.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend supabase/functions/apify-webhook/index.ts with the ig_details case per docs/tech-architecture.md §3
Updates the IG-related denorm columns only (does not touch tiktok or ig_posts fields)
Test passes for a synthetic ig_details payload
Commit

userStories.title: Edge function: apify-webhook (ig_posts branch)
userStories.description: IG posts branch — populates instagram_avg_views_last_10 etc.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend webhook with ig_posts case
Computes the rolling 10-post avg per spec
Test passes
Commit

userStories.title: Edge function: auth-signup-creator (handle inputs, no scrape yet)
userStories.description: Creator signup that records handles but does not yet kick scrapes — those come in US-020.
acceptanceCriteria: supabase/functions/auth-signup-creator/index.ts: POST { username, tiktok_handle?, instagram_handle? } → 200 { token, user_id, role }
Requires at least one handle; 422 if both missing
Inserts users + creator_profiles + social_links rows in a single transaction
Signs and returns JWT
Round-trip test verified
Commit

userStories.title: Wire signup-creator to kick Apify scrapes (waitSecs=60)
userStories.description: After insert, fire up to three Apify runs synchronously with a 60s wait; webhook handles late completions.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend auth-signup-creator to call runTikTokProfile / runInstagramDetails / runInstagramPosts per the handles supplied
Use Promise.allSettled — partial failure is acceptable (some metrics are better than none)
Within waitSecs the response includes a `metrics_status: { tiktok, ig_details, ig_posts }` map of statuses
Test against a real signup with a known live TikTok/IG handle and confirm metric_snapshots rows appear within 60s
Commit

userStories.title: Edge function: metrics-refresh with 6h throttle
userStories.description: POST /metrics/refresh body { platform: 'tiktok'|'instagram' } — kicks scrapes if last refresh >6h ago.
acceptanceCriteria: supabase/functions/metrics-refresh/index.ts: requires JWT; reads (user_id, platform) from body+claims
Checks the latest metric_snapshots.created_at for that pair; if <6h, returns 429 with { retry_after_seconds }
Otherwise kicks the appropriate Apify run(s)
Test: two back-to-back calls — second returns 429
Commit

userStories.title: Design tokens module (colors, spacing, radii, shadows)
userStories.description: Per docs/design.md §3 — single source of truth for visual tokens.
acceptanceCriteria: src/design/tokens.ts exports: colors (full palette including pink #EC4899, orange #F97316, ink, canvas #FFF9F2, semantic statuses + soft-bg variants), spacing (4/8/12/16/20/24/32/48), radii (12 card, 999 pill, 4 input), shadows (hard 2px ink offset)
Unit type test (compile-time): typed const objects with `as const`
tsc passes
Commit

userStories.title: Typography setup (Clash Display, Satoshi, JetBrains Mono)
userStories.description: Load custom fonts via expo-font; expose typed text style presets.
acceptanceCriteria: Fonts loaded via expo-font in app root; splash held until loaded
src/design/typography.ts exports textStyles: display, h1, h2, body, caption, mono — each with fontFamily, fontSize, lineHeight from docs/design.md
If font assets are not committed, use the Expo Google Fonts equivalent for Clash/Satoshi (or fall back to Inter + JetBrains Mono) and add a `Spec gap:` note flagging the asset substitution
Commit

userStories.title: Motion presets + useReducedMotion wrapper
userStories.description: Three spring presets (soft/snappy/bouncy) + a hook that respects OS reduced-motion.
acceptanceCriteria: src/design/motion.ts exports SPRING_SOFT/SPRING_SNAPPY/SPRING_BOUNCY config objects per docs/design.md §6
src/design/useReducedMotion.ts hook wraps AccessibilityInfo and returns boolean
Helper `withReducedMotion(animation, reduced)` returns a no-op animation when reduced=true
Commit

userStories.title: Primitive: ButtonPrimary + ButtonSecondary
userStories.description: Press-collapse shadow per docs/design.md.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/primitives/Button.tsx exports ButtonPrimary, ButtonSecondary
Variants render the orange CTA (primary) and ink-bordered secondary
Press-in collapses the 2px hard shadow via Reanimated worklet (snappy spring); haptic on press
Disabled state grays out and disables haptic
Mobile-mcp verification: render a screen showing both buttons, tap each, screenshot the press state
Commit

userStories.title: Primitive: Chip + StatusPill
userStories.description: Pill-shaped chips for filters and status badges with the four semantic colors.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/primitives/Chip.tsx + StatusPill.tsx
Chip is interactive (selectable); StatusPill is read-only with semantic color from prop
Mobile-mcp verification: render screen with all four status pills + chips in selected/unselected, screenshot
Commit

userStories.title: Primitive: CampaignCard + SkeletonCard
userStories.description: The hero card used on the discover feed.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/primitives/CampaignCard.tsx renders title, lister handle, price (display-only), pre-condition summary, status pill
Press-collapse shadow on tap
SkeletonCard with shimmer (Reanimated worklet) for loading state
Mobile-mcp verification: screen with two cards + one skeleton, screenshot
Commit

userStories.title: Primitive: BottomSheet + Toast
userStories.description: Sheet for filters/forms; toast for success/error transient feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/primitives/BottomSheet.tsx — gesture-driven dismissal, snap points, scrim
src/components/primitives/Toast.tsx — global toast provider in app root, success/error/info variants, auto-dismiss with progress bar
Mobile-mcp verification: open sheet, dismiss via drag, fire toast — screenshot each
Commit

userStories.title: Storybook-style preview screen for primitives
userStories.description: Single-screen catalog of all primitives for QA and future iterations.
acceptanceCriteria: src/screens/_dev/PrimitivesPreview.tsx renders every primitive with realistic props
Reachable via expo-router at /_dev/primitives (only registered in __DEV__)
Mobile-mcp verification: navigate to the route, screenshot
Commit

userStories.title: Supabase client + auth context (JWT in MMKV)
userStories.description: Wire the typed Supabase client and an auth context that persists JWT in MMKV.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/supabase.ts exports a typed createClient() using src/types/supabase.ts; reads SUPABASE_URL + SUPABASE_ANON_KEY from expo-constants
Custom Authorization header injected from MMKV-stored JWT; refresh on auth context change
src/lib/auth.tsx provides AuthProvider + useAuth() with { user, role, signIn(token, user), signOut() }
JWT stored under MMKV key 'marketify.jwt'
Commit

userStories.title: Role-based router (creator vs lister tabs)
userStories.description: Expo-router stack with role-aware tab layouts per docs/design.md §6.
acceptanceCriteria: app/_layout.tsx wraps with AuthProvider + ToastProvider + GestureHandlerRootView
Unauthed users see (auth) group; creators see (creator) tab group with Discover/Applied/Submitted/Profile; listers see (lister) tab group with Home/Campaigns/Inbox/Profile + a FAB hook
All tab labels + lucide icons per docs/design.md
Mobile-mcp verification: launch unauthed → see auth screen; manually call signIn() with a creator token via dev menu → tabs switch; same for lister
Commit

userStories.title: Onboarding role-picker screen
userStories.description: Initial screen letting users pick creator or lister.
acceptanceCriteria: app/(auth)/index.tsx renders the role picker per docs/design.md (two large pressables, ink borders, hard shadows)
Tap routes to /(auth)/signup/creator or /(auth)/signup/lister
Mobile-mcp verification: launch fresh app, screenshot, tap each, confirm navigation
Commit

userStories.title: Lister signup screen
userStories.description: Username + email + org name → calls auth-signup-lister edge function.
acceptanceCriteria: app/(auth)/signup/lister.tsx — three TextInput fields with inline validation
Submit calls supabase.functions.invoke('auth-signup-lister'); on success, signIn() then router.replace('/(lister)') and toast 'Welcome'
On 409 conflict: show inline error on the conflicting field
Mobile-mcp verification: complete signup with a fresh username, screenshot success state
Commit

userStories.title: Creator signup screen with handle inputs
userStories.description: Username + tiktok_handle and/or instagram_handle.
acceptanceCriteria: app/(auth)/signup/creator.tsx with handle fields rendered in JetBrains Mono
Validates at least one handle is supplied
Submit calls auth-signup-creator; loading state shows 'Pulling your stats…' with skeleton metrics for up to 60s
On success: signIn() then router.replace('/(creator)') and toast
Mobile-mcp verification: signup with a real handle, screenshot the loading + landed states
Commit

userStories.title: Creator profile screen — handles + metrics + add/unlink
userStories.description: Shows linked handles + denormalized metrics, supports adding/removing handles.
acceptanceCriteria: app/(creator)/profile.tsx queries creator_profiles + social_links
Renders TikTok + IG sections with monospace handles, follower counts, avg views, last_refreshed_at relative time
Add Handle / Unlink Handle actions wired to a small edge function `manage-social-link` (create that function as part of this story)
'Stale' chip appears when metrics_stale_at is non-null
Mobile-mcp verification: navigate to profile, screenshot
Commit

userStories.title: Pull-to-refresh metrics on creator profile (6h throttle)
userStories.description: Drag-to-refresh per platform calls metrics-refresh edge function.
acceptanceCriteria: Each platform card has its own pull-to-refresh; on release calls metrics-refresh for that platform
On 429: show toast 'Try again in Xh Ym'
On success: optimistic last_refreshed_at update; subscribe to metric_snapshots realtime to update numbers when webhook lands
Mobile-mcp verification: drag, observe loading, screenshot
Commit

userStories.title: Lister profile screen
userStories.description: Org name, email, sign-out.
acceptanceCriteria: app/(lister)/profile.tsx renders lister_profiles fields + sign-out button
Sign-out clears MMKV and routes to (auth)
Mobile-mcp verification: screenshot, tap sign-out, screenshot
Commit

userStories.title: Discover feed screen with eligibility filter toggle
userStories.description: Direct PostgREST query with the cache columns; client-side fine-grained eligibility for the rest.
acceptanceCriteria: app/(creator)/discover.tsx queries listings WHERE status='active' AND min_followers_tiktok <= creator.tiktok_follower_count (and IG equivalent) when 'Eligible only' toggle is on (default on)
Renders CampaignCard list; pull-to-refresh; SkeletonCard while loading
Toggle in header switches eligibility filter, persisted in MMKV
Empty state per docs/design.md when zero results
Mobile-mcp verification: load feed (seeded data), toggle filter, screenshot both states
Commit

userStories.title: Edge function: get-listing-detail
userStories.description: Returns listing + conditions + live eligibility for the calling creator.
acceptanceCriteria: supabase/functions/get-listing-detail/index.ts: GET (or POST per Supabase func conventions) with listing_id; requires creator JWT
Returns { listing, pre_conditions, post_conditions, sample_videos, eligibility: { eligible: boolean, reasons: string[] } }
Eligibility engine logic per docs/tech-architecture.md (eligibility section)
Test: seeded eligible creator → eligible:true; force-fail one threshold → eligible:false with reason
Commit

userStories.title: Campaign detail screen + eligibility evaluation
userStories.description: Tap a card → full detail with apply CTA gated on eligibility.
acceptanceCriteria: app/(creator)/listing/[id].tsx fetches via get-listing-detail
Renders title, description, price (display-only), pre/post conditions, sample videos as TikTok/IG links
If eligible: ButtonPrimary 'Apply'; if not: greyed CTA with bullet list of failed reasons
Mobile-mcp verification: open eligible + ineligible listings (seed both), screenshot each
Commit

userStories.title: Edge function: apply-to-listing (server-side re-check)
userStories.description: Re-evaluates eligibility, then inserts an applications row.
acceptanceCriteria: supabase/functions/apply-to-listing/index.ts: POST { listing_id, pitch? } → 200 { application_id } or 422 { reasons }
Re-runs eligibility engine; rejects if not eligible
Inserts applications row in status=pending, links to listing's current version
Test passes
Commit

userStories.title: Apply modal on campaign detail
userStories.description: Bottom sheet with optional pitch field → calls apply-to-listing.
acceptanceCriteria: Tapping Apply opens BottomSheet with pitch TextInput (optional, 280 char limit) and submit
On 200: toast success + close sheet + navigate to (creator)/applied tab
On 422: toast with first reason
Mobile-mcp verification: open sheet, submit, screenshot success
Commit

userStories.title: My Applications tab (creator)
userStories.description: Lists creator's applications grouped by status.
acceptanceCriteria: app/(creator)/applied.tsx queries applications joined to listings
Tabs/segments: Pending / Approved / Rejected / Cancelled
Each row: campaign title, lister handle, status pill, applied_at relative
Tap row → navigates to listing detail
Mobile-mcp verification: screenshot each segment with seeded data
Commit

userStories.title: Realtime subscription for application status changes (creator)
userStories.description: Push status flips into the UI via Supabase Realtime.
acceptanceCriteria: Subscribe to applications WHERE creator_user_id = me on the Applied tab
On UPDATE: optimistically move the row to the new segment; fire toast 'Your application was approved/rejected'
Mobile-mcp verification: from Supabase Studio (or via execute_sql) flip a seeded application to approved while the screen is open — confirm UI updates without manual refresh
Commit

userStories.title: Edge function: submit-video
userStories.description: Creator pastes a video URL after approval; oEmbed validates ownership-ish + URL shape.
acceptanceCriteria: supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts: POST { application_id, video_url, post_condition_affirmations: { [pc_id]: boolean } }
Validates URL is a TikTok or IG post URL via regex per docs/tech-architecture.md
Calls TikTok/IG oEmbed (no auth required) to confirm the URL resolves and capture thumbnail + author handle (warn if author handle != creator's handle, but do not block)
Inserts submissions row in status=pending
All post_conditions must have an affirmation: true; otherwise 422
Test passes
Commit

userStories.title: Submission composer screen
userStories.description: URL paste → oEmbed preview → post-condition checklist → submit.
acceptanceCriteria: app/(creator)/submit/[applicationId].tsx with URL input (mono), live oEmbed preview (thumbnail + caption snippet), checklist for post-conditions
Submit button disabled until URL valid + all conditions checked
On success: toast + route to Submitted tab
Mobile-mcp verification: paste a real TikTok URL, screenshot preview + submission
Commit

userStories.title: My Submissions tab (creator)
userStories.description: Pending / Approved / Rejected segments.
acceptanceCriteria: app/(creator)/submitted.tsx mirrors the applied tab structure for submissions
Realtime subscription on submissions WHERE creator_user_id = me
Mobile-mcp verification: screenshot each segment
Commit

userStories.title: Lister Home/dashboard screen
userStories.description: Quick stats + entry points.
acceptanceCriteria: app/(lister)/index.tsx shows counts: active campaigns, pending applications across all campaigns, pending submissions across all campaigns
FAB on this screen routes to create-campaign wizard step 1
Mobile-mcp verification: screenshot
Commit

userStories.title: Create-campaign wizard step 1 — basics
userStories.description: Title, description, price (display-only), currency.
acceptanceCriteria: app/(lister)/create/step-1.tsx with title, description, price (number), currency (USD/EUR/GBP)
Stores draft in zustand or react-context wizard store + MMKV
Next button enabled when title + description present
Mobile-mcp verification: fill, navigate next, screenshot
Commit

userStories.title: Create-campaign wizard step 2 — pre-conditions
userStories.description: Add/remove pre-condition rows (platform + metric + threshold).
acceptanceCriteria: step-2 screen lets the lister add rows: platform (TikTok/IG), metric (followers, avg_views), operator (>=), threshold
Saves to wizard store
Mobile-mcp verification: add two rows, screenshot
Commit

userStories.title: Create-campaign wizard step 3 — post-conditions
userStories.description: Free-text rules creators must self-affirm at submit time.
acceptanceCriteria: step-3 screen: list of post-condition strings (add/remove)
Each gets an internal id (uuid) so submission affirmations can map to it
Mobile-mcp verification: add three, screenshot

userStories.title: Create-campaign wizard step 4 — samples + max submissions
userStories.description: Sample video URLs (TikTok/IG only — no upload) + max_submissions integer.
acceptanceCriteria: step-4 screen: add/remove sample URLs (validated regex), max_submissions number input
Mobile-mcp verification: add two URLs, set max=10, screenshot
Commit

userStories.title: Create-campaign wizard step 5 — review + publish
userStories.description: Show creator-side preview (using CampaignCard + detail layout) and Publish CTA.
acceptanceCriteria: step-5 renders the listing as a creator would see it

Publish button calls a new edge function `create-listing` (write that function as part of this story) which inserts listings + listing_pre_conditions + listing_post_conditions in a single transaction and bumps the cache columns
On success: clear MMKV draft, toast, route to (lister)/campaigns
Mobile-mcp verification: end-to-end create flow, screenshot the published listing in the lister's My Campaigns

userStories.title: My Campaigns screen (lister)
userStories.description: Active vs Inactive segments.
acceptanceCriteria: app/(lister)/campaigns.tsx queries listings WHERE lister_user_id = me
Two segments: Active / Inactive (status)
Each row uses CampaignCard with extra meta (applications count, submissions count)
Mobile-mcp verification: screenshot
Commit

userStories.title: Edit campaign + cascade-cancel confirmation modal
userStories.description: Editing versioned fields shows the cascade impact before saving.
acceptanceCriteria: Tap a campaign on My Campaigns → edit screen pre-filled
Before saving versioned fields, query count of pending applications via execute_sql (or a small RPC) and show modal: 'This will cancel N pending applications. Continue?'
On confirm: PATCH listing — the DB trigger handles the cascade
Mobile-mcp verification: edit a seeded listing's price, see the modal with the seeded application count, confirm, then verify in My Applications that the cascade fired
Commit

userStories.title: Lister Inbox: Applications tab
userStories.description: Pending/Approved/Rejected applications across all owned campaigns, with creator metrics surfaced.
acceptanceCriteria: app/(lister)/inbox/applications.tsx queries applications joined to listings + creator_profiles
Each row shows creator handles (mono), TikTok followers, IG followers + avg views, application pitch preview
Realtime subscription for inserts
Mobile-mcp verification: screenshot
Commit

userStories.title: Application review with eligibility re-check + override dialog
userStories.description: Approve re-evaluates eligibility against current creator metrics; if drifted, show OverrideEligibilityDialog.
acceptanceCriteria: Tap a pending application → review sheet with creator metrics + pitch
Approve button triggers a new edge function `decide-application` (write it now): re-runs eligibility, returns { ok: true } or { ok: false, drift: string[] } so the UI can render the override dialog
Override confirms with second tap → application becomes approved regardless
Reject button + reason field
Mobile-mcp verification: approve a seeded application, then approve one with drifted metrics (manually update the creator_profiles to drop a count) — screenshot both flows
Commit

userStories.title: Lister Inbox: Submissions tab
userStories.description: Pending submissions across all owned campaigns.
acceptanceCriteria: app/(lister)/inbox/submissions.tsx queries submissions joined to applications + listings
Realtime subscription
ReuseBadge: when video_url appears in N>1 submissions, show 'also submitted to N other campaigns'
Mobile-mcp verification: screenshot
Commit

userStories.title: Submission review screen — embedded video + checklist
userStories.description: Lister reviews the submitted URL with embedded WebView and checks each post-condition.
acceptanceCriteria: app/(lister)/inbox/submissions/[id].tsx with WebView embedding the TikTok/IG URL (oEmbed embed URL)
Checklist of the listing's post-conditions (read-only at this stage; lister mentally verifies + ticks)
Approve / Reject + feedback text
Decision via edge function `decide-submission` (write as part of this story) which updates submissions and inserts a notification for the creator
Mobile-mcp verification: screenshot review state, approve, screenshot success animation
Commit

userStories.title: Approval / rejection animations (celebration + shake)
userStories.description: Wire the celebration burst on approval and shake on rejection per docs/design.md §6.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/effects/CelebrationBurst.tsx + ShakeOnError.tsx using Reanimated worklets
Triggered on creator side when an application or submission flips to approved/rejected (Realtime push)
Respects useReducedMotion
Mobile-mcp verification: trigger an approval via execute_sql while the creator screen is open — record screenshots of celebration
Commit

userStories.title: In-app notifications inbox + bell badge
userStories.description: Notifications tab + a bell badge visible on every screen header.
acceptanceCriteria: app/(creator)/notifications.tsx and app/(lister)/notifications.tsx (or shared component) list notifications rows with unread first
Bell badge in shared header counts unread; tap routes to inbox
Tap notification marks read + deep-links to the relevant entity (application/submission/listing)
Realtime subscription on notifications WHERE recipient_user_id = me
Mobile-mcp verification: trigger a few notifications via execute_sql, see badge count tick, tap, screenshot
Commit

userStories.title: Empty states for every list screen
userStories.description: Per docs/design.md — illustrated empty states with CTA where applicable.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/shared/EmptyState.tsx with title, body, optional CTA
Wired into Discover, Applied, Submitted, Campaigns, Inbox sub-tabs, Notifications
Mobile-mcp verification: sign in as a fresh user with no data, screenshot each empty state
Commit

userStories.title: Error states + retry on every screen
userStories.description: Network errors should never strand the user.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/shared/ErrorState.tsx with title, body, Retry button
All edge function callers wrap fetch with try/catch and surface an ErrorState in the loading slot when failed
Toast on transient errors (e.g. 429, 5xx)
Mobile-mcp verification: kill connectivity (Airplane mode in simulator), pull-to-refresh discover, screenshot error state, restore connectivity, retry, screenshot success
Commit

userStories.title: Reduced-motion audit
userStories.description: Verify every animated component honors useReducedMotion.
acceptanceCriteria: Grep for `useSharedValue` / `withSpring` / `withTiming` and confirm each touchpoint either uses withReducedMotion helper or short-circuits when reduced
Toggle Reduce Motion in the iOS simulator (Settings → Accessibility) and verify CelebrationBurst, ShakeOnError, button press animations no-op
Mobile-mcp verification: screenshot interactions with reduced motion enabled — confirm static visuals
Append findings + any fixes to progress.txt
Commit

userStories.title: ReuseBadge surfaced on lister submission rows
userStories.description: Already wired in US-058; this story adds polish + tap behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Tap the badge opens a sheet listing the other campaigns this URL was submitted to (titles only)
Mobile-mcp verification: with a seeded duplicate-URL submission, tap the badge, screenshot the sheet
Commit
